questions.question: What is the 'blocking problem' in the Two-Phase Commit protocol? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: It occurs if the coordinator fails after participants vote COMMIT but before sending the final decision.
Participants are left in an uncertain state and cannot proceed (are blocked) until the coordinator recovers, as they do not know the outcome.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Two-Phase Commit (2PC) and Three-Phase Commit (3PC) protocols. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: 2PC has two phases (Voting, Decision) while 3PC has three (CanCommit?, PreCommit, DoCommit).
2PC is a blocking protocol; participants block if the coordinator fails. 3PC is non-blocking due to the PreCommit phase.
2PC has lower message latency (2 rounds) compared to 3PC (3 rounds).
2PC is simpler to implement and is widely used.
3PC requires a synchronous network model assumption, while 2PC is more practical for asynchronous networks.
